data.title: Insteel GAAP EPS of $0.52 beats by $0.25, revenue of $160.66M beats by $10.81M
data.description:
• Insteel press release (NYSE:IIIN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.52 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $160.66M (+26.1% Y/Y) beats by $10.81M.
  • Insteel ended the quarter with $28.4 million of cash and no borrowings outstanding on its $100.0 million revolving credit facility.
  • Improving customer demand and cautiously positive market outlook

  • Outlook

    “We are encouraged by the strengthening demand for our products that began during our first quarter,” said H.O. Woltz III, President and CEO of Insteel. “The positive momentum we experienced through our second fiscal quarter was driven by improving business conditions and rising customer confidence, trends that are not fully reflected in the broader macroeconomic indices generally used to measure the strength of the construction industry. It is important to note that our results, and my comments, reflect conditions we experienced prior to the recent turmoil created by the changing tariff policy of the Trump Administration. Nevertheless, as we move into the second half of fiscal 2025, we are encouraged by the pace of activity in our markets and believe it is likely to continue through the summer and into the fall."

data.title: Former CISA chief resigns from SentinelOne amid alleged pressure from White House
data.description:
Christopher Krebs, whom President Donald Trump removed as head of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency in 2020, said he has resigned from SentinelOne (NYSE:S), effective April 16, following alleged pressure from the White House.

Krebs, who served as chief intelligence and public policy officer at cybersecurity company SentinelOne, said in an email to employees that it was his "decision, and mine alone."

"As many of you know, when the news broke last week about the Executive Order — one that directly named me and referenced  SentinelOne — I immediately reached out to Tomer [CEO Tomer Weingarten]. I told him what I firmly believe: this is my fight, not the company's, and I offered my resignation," said Krebs in the email.

In an April 9, executive order, Trump directed the Attorney General, the Director of National Intelligence, and all other relevant agencies to immediately take all action as necessary "to suspend any active security clearances held by individuals at entities associated with Krebs, including SentinelOne."

Trump had removed Krebs from CISA in November 2020 after he refused to endorse the president's claim that the election had been rigged.

The April 9 executive order alleged that "Krebs, through CISA, falsely and baselessly denied that the 2020 election was rigged and stolen, including by inappropriately and categorically dismissing widespread election malfeasance and serious vulnerabilities with voting machines."

SentinelOne had said on April 9 that it would continue to nurture its partnerships in the U.S. government, military, and intelligence community and collaborate in "our shared mission to defend U.S. critical infrastructure in cyberspace as we always have."

data.title: D.R. Horton GAAP EPS of $2.58 misses by $0.07, revenue of $7.73B misses by $300M
data.description:
• D.R. Horton press release (NYSE:DHI): Q2 GAAP EPS of $2.58 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $7.73B (-15.1% Y/Y) misses by $300M.
  • Homes sales revenues of $7.2 billion on 19,276 homes closed
  • Net sales orders of 22,437 homes with an order value of $8.4 billion
  • New share repurchase authorization of $5.0 billion
  • 2025 Guidance
  • Consolidated revenues in the range of $33.3 billion to $34.8 billion vs $36.14B consensus
  • Homes closed by homebuilding operations of 85,000 homes to 87,000 homes
  • Consolidated cash flow provided by operations of greater than $3.0 billion
  • Share repurchases of approximately $4.0 billion

  • The Company is reiterating its fiscal 2025 guidance as follows:

    • Income tax rate of approximately 24.0%
    • Dividend payments of approximately $500 million

data.title: Snap-On misses Q1 top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Snap-On press release (NYSE:SNA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $4.51 misses by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $1.14B (-3.4% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • The first quarter effective income tax rate was 22.2% in both 2025 and 2024.

  • Outlook: "We believe that our markets and our operations possess and have demonstrated continuing and considerable resilience against the uncertainties of the current environment. In 2025, Snap-on expects to make ongoing progress along its decisive runways for coherent growth, leveraging capabilities already proven in the automotive repair arena, developing and expanding its professional customer base, not only in automotive repair, but in adjacent markets, additional geographies and other areas, including extending in critical industries, where the cost and penalties for failure can be high. In pursuit of these initiatives, we project that capital expenditures in 2025 will approximate $100 million, of which $22.9 million was incurred in the first three months of the year."

  • Snap-on currently anticipates that its full-year 2025 effective income tax rate will be in the range of 22% to 23%.

data.title: UnitedHealth slumps, drags peers after guidance cut, Q1 miss
data.description:
• Dow component UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) fell ~20% in the premarket on Thursday after the managed care giant and industry bellwether in the earnings season slashed its full-year outlook following lower-than-expected Q1 2025 financials.
  • Its peers also traded lower in reaction. Notable underperformers include UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (NYSE:CI), Clover Health (CLOV), and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC).
  • Medicaid-leveraged managed care companies Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) also trended lower.
  • “UnitedHealth Group grew to serve more people more comprehensively but did not perform up to our expectations,” CEO Andrew Witty remarked before the conference call at 8:45 a.m. EST. “We are aggressively addressing those challenges to position us well for the years ahead and return to our long-term earnings growth rate target of 13 to 16%.”

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Eureka Lithium looks to raise C$400K in private placement
data.description:
• Eureka Lithium (OTCQB:UREKF) said it intends to conduct a non-brokered private placement offering of units at a price of C$0.0825 apiece, for gross proceeds of ~C$400K.
  • Each unit will have one share and one share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one share at a price of C$0.11 per share for a period of 24 months following issuance.
  • The firm expects to use the proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: UnitedHealth in charts: Q1 UnitedHealthcare revenue rises 12.3% Y/Y; Optum revenue up 4.7% Y/Y
Earnings Snapshot: UnitedHealth Group lowers FY2025 EPS outlook after missing Q1 earnings
Russia prepares for return of foreign companies, but conditions apply
data.description:
Russia is working on a legal framework for foreign companies to re-enter the country, amid hopes that Western sanctions will eventually be lifted. But many companies are wary of making concrete plans to return. 

"They're all doing due diligence," Chris Weafer, who runs business consultancy Macro Advisory, told Bloomberg. But most companies wouldn't consider returning till there is a permanent peace deal, as "nobody wants to come back too soon and then have to exit again," Weafer added. 

Russia's finance ministry has been drafting conditions for foreign companies seeking to return, which reportedly include requirements for localizing some production and agreeing to technology transfers. Russian businesses would also be given a say on companies returning to certain sectors.

Many companies sold their Russian subsidiaries after Moscow's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The deals included buyback clauses, which would enable them to reclaim the assets at a cheaper price, although this is unlikely to happen.

"All new investors who want to come to Russia will receive the most favorable working conditions," Anatoly Aksakov, head of the financial markets committee in Russia's lower house of parliament, told Bloomberg. 

But companies will have to pay full market prices to reclaim the assets they sold when they decided to exit the country, Aksakov added.

Italian heating systems maker Ariston is set to restart operations in Russia after it was given back control of its local unit. The subsidiary had been seized and placed under the temporary control of Russia's Gazprom. 

South Korea's LG Electronics recently restarted its operations at its plant near Moscow. Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) are assessing the feasibility of operating in a post-war Russian market, The Moscow Times reported last month. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Regions Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.78B misses by $50M
data.description:
• Regions Financial press release (NYSE:RF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.78B (+1.7% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • Adjusted total revenue of $1.81B.
